name: ml-training
description: ML model training workflows with PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM. Includes experiment tracking, hyperparameter tuning, and model evaluation.
author: anwar

commands.name: ml:train
commands.description: Train ML model with experiment tracking
commands.prompt:
Create a production-ready ML training pipeline with:
- Data validation and preprocessing (Great Expectations / pandera)
- Feature engineering pipeline (Feature-engine / custom transformers)
- Model training with PyTorch Lightning / PyTorch / scikit-learn / XGBoost / LightGBM
- Experiment tracking with MLflow / Weights & Biases / ClearML
- Hyperparameter optimization with Optuna / Ray Tune / Hyperopt
- Model evaluation with proper metrics, calibration, and fairness checks
- Model serialization with MLflow / ONNX / TorchScript
- Reproducibility: config management (Hydra/OmegaConf), seeds, environment capture
- Type hints, docstrings, logging, error handling

arguments.name: model_type
arguments.description: Model type: pytorch, sklearn, xgboost, lightgbm, tabpfn

commands.name: ml:eval
commands.prompt:
Create comprehensive model evaluation with:
- Metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, log-loss, calibration
- Per-class and macro/micro/weighted averages
- Confusion matrix, ROC/PR curves, calibration plots
- Feature importance: SHAP, permutation importance, permutation feature importance
- Error analysis: misclassified examples, confidence distributions
- Fairness metrics: demographic parity, equal opportunity, disparate impact
- Robustness: adversarial examples, distribution shift detection
- Model cards generation with model card toolkit
- Comparison baseline models
- Statistical significance testing

commands.name: ml:hpo
commands.prompt:
Set up hyperparameter optimization with:
- Search space definition (Optuna distributions / Ray Tune search spaces)
- Multi-objective optimization (accuracy vs latency vs size)
- Pruning: MedianPruner, Hyperband, ASHA
- Distributed optimization with Ray Tune / Optuna distributed
- Early stopping integration
- Visualization: optimization history, parameter importance, slice plots
- Best trial retraining with full budget
- Configuration management with Hydra

commands.name: ml:tabular
commands.description: Tabular ML pipeline with modern libraries
commands.prompt:
Create tabular ML pipeline with:
- Polars for fast data processing
- Feature engineering: target encoding, frequency encoding, embeddings for categorical
- Gradient boosting: XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, TabPFN
- Tabular deep learning: TabNet, TabTransformer, FT-Transformer, Saint
- AutoML: AutoGluon, FLAML, H2O
- Cross-validation: StratifiedKFold, GroupKFold, PurgedKFold
- Pseudo-labeling, stacking, blending
- Feature selection: SHAP-based, permutation, mutual information
- Handling: missing values, outliers, class imbalance, target leakage
- Interpretability: SHAP, LIME, EBM

arguments.name: algorithm
arguments.description: xgboost, lightgbm, catboost, tabpfn, autogluon, tabnet

agents.name: ml-engineer
agents.description: ML engineer for end-to-end model development
agents.prompt: You are a senior ML engineer. Build production-ready ML systems with: clean architecture, modular code, config-driven experiments, comprehensive testing, CI/CD integration, monitoring, and documentation. Use modern stack: PyTorch Lightning, Hydra, MLflow, Optuna, Polars, Ruff, PyTest. Follow MLOps best practices: reproducibility, lineage, governance.
